NOT REPORTED. COMPLEX LITIGATION PLAINTIFF'S MOTION RE RES JUDICATA PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED ON MAY-14-2025. THE PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A PROPOSED SEVENTH AMENDED COMPLAINT ADDING SANCHEZ AS A PLAINTIFF AND PAGA REPRESENTATIVE IS DENIED AS FUTILE. FURTHER, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S TENTH THROUGH THIRTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST ADECCO AND THE BULK OF PLAINTIFF'S NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE DOCTRINE OF CLAIM PRECLUSION FOLLOWING SETTLEMENT IN MONIZ, WHICH DEPRIVES CORREA OF STANDING TO MAINTAIN THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST ADECCO. FINALLY, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT ADDING MARISOL SANCHEZ AS A NAMED PLAINTIFF AND PAGA REPRESENTATIVE WOULD NOT RELATE BACK TO THE MARCH 23, 2017 SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT'S RULING ISSUED ON MAY-15-2025 ORDER. NOTICE SENT BY COURT. (D304)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS GOOGLE, INC. AND ALPHABET, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Off calendar for department 302. The case has or soon will be designated complex and single assigned to one of the complex departments. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kelley De Young, CSR #6780 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Continued To Apr-07-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chris Baker, of Baker Curtis & Schwartz, P.C., Tel. 415.433.1064, for plaintiffs;
Cameron W. Fox, of Paul Hastings LLP, Tel. 213.683.6301, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The parties discuss the Court's disclosure. Although both parties consider waiving the conflict pursuant to CCP 170.3, the Court recuses himself from hearing the matter. DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC., ALPHABET, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to April 7, 2017, (control date only), to check the status of the application for complex case designation. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The demurrer has been mooted by the filing of the first amended complaint. Google's position, based solely on two federal trial court decisions, that its demurrer should be ruled on is contrary to California law. If, as Google argues in its reply memorandum, the issues raised in its demurrer apply equally to the first amended complaint, Google's remedy is to raise those issues in a demurrer to the first amended complaint.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported. (D302)
